features.name: Streak Tracking
features.description: Track the number of consecutive days a user completes at least one review session. Expose current streak and longest streak on the dashboard and profile.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see my daily streak so that I am motivated to open the app every day.
acceptance_criteria: Streak counter increments when at least one /srs/review submission is recorded on a calendar day.
Streak resets to 0 if no review is submitted on a calendar day.
Current streak and longest streak are visible on the profile/dashboard page.
A streak-shield token can be earned (e.g., via a milestone) and prevents one reset.
backend_changes: Add currentStreak (number), longestStreak (number), lastReviewDate (Date) fields to User schema.
Update streak counters inside the POST /srs/review handler after each submission.
Expose streak values via GET /users/me or GET /srs/progress.
frontend_changes: Display streak flame icon + counter in nav bar or dashboard.
Show streak milestone animation when streak increments.

features.name: Daily XP Goal & Experience Points
features.description: Award XP for each correct answer submitted via the SRS review endpoint. Let users choose a daily XP goal. Show a progress bar towards today's goal on the home screen.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to set a daily XP target and see my progress toward it so that I have a clear, achievable goal each day.
acceptance_criteria: Correct answers award 10 XP each; incorrect answers award 4 XP (for participation).
Users can select a daily goal: Casual (20 XP), Regular (50 XP), Serious (100 XP), Intense (200 XP).
The home screen shows a progress bar: today's XP earned vs daily goal.
Reaching the daily goal triggers a congratulatory animation.
XP history is queryable by day/week for the dashboard chart.
backend_changes: Add xpEarned (number) to each UserWordProgress review event.
Add dailyXpGoal (number) to User schema.
Add GET /srs/xp?period=today|week|month endpoint returning aggregated XP.
frontend_changes: XP goal selector in onboarding and settings.
Daily XP progress bar on home/dashboard.
XP earned summary on quiz results screen.

features.name: Spaced Repetition UI Integration
features.description: Wire the existing SM-2 backend to the frontend. The quiz should load due cards from GET /srs/due and submit results to POST /srs/review after each answer. A 'Review Due' card on the home screen shows how many words need practice today.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want the app to show me words I am about to forget so that my study time is as efficient as possible.
acceptance_criteria: VocabularyProvider fetches words from GET /srs/due instead of hardcoded localStorage data.
After each answer in the quiz, POST /srs/review is called with wordId, quality (0-5), and responseTime.
The home screen displays a badge showing the count of words due for review today.
If no words are due, the home screen says 'All caught up!' and offers to practice ahead.
The quiz gracefully handles the empty-due-list state.
frontend_changes: Refactor VocabularyProvider to fetch from /srs/due via authFetch.
Add review submission call inside useTimedQuizMachine after SELECT_ANSWER action.
Add 'due today' count badge to home page using GET /srs/due response length.
Add loading and error states to VocabularyProvider.

features.name: Multiple Exercise Types
features.description: Introduce three additional exercise formats alongside the existing multiple-choice quiz: tap-the-pairs (matching), free-text translation input, and word-bank sentence ordering.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want varied question formats so that I am challenged in different ways and do not get bored.
acceptance_criteria: Tap-the-pairs: two columns (Uzbek / English) of 4-6 items each; user taps matching pairs; correct pairs disappear.
Free-text input: a word is shown; user types the translation; fuzzy matching accepts minor typos.
Word-bank ordering: a target sentence is shown with one word blanked; user selects from 4 word-bank options.
Exercise type is randomly selected per question within a session, weighted by the word's current SRS interval.
All three types submit results to the same POST /srs/review endpoint.
backend_changes: Add exerciseType field to the review submission payload (multiple_choice | pairs | typing | word_bank).
Optionally adjust quality score weighting based on exercise difficulty.
frontend_changes: New MatchingPairs component.
New TypingExercise component with fuzzy match utility.
New WordBank component.
Update useTimedQuizMachine to support exerciseType state and dispatch.

features.name: Vocabulary Organised by Topic / Skill Tree
features.description: Group words into thematic skill units (e.g., Greetings, Numbers, Food, Family, Travel). Users progress through units sequentially; completing a unit unlocks the next. A visual grid shows unit completion status.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to study vocabulary grouped by topic so that I can see my overall progress and know what to study next.
acceptance_criteria: Words have an optional topic field (string, e.g., 'greetings', 'food').
A Skill model exists with: id, name, description, iconUrl, prerequisiteSkillIds[], wordIds[].
The home page shows a skill grid; locked skills are greyed out.
A skill unlocks when all prerequisite skills have at least 80% of words at SRS interval >= 7 days.
Clicking an unlocked skill starts a review session filtered to that skill's words.
backend_changes: Add topic (string) field to Word schema.
Create Skill schema with name, description, iconUrl, prerequisiteSkillIds, wordIds.
Create SkillsModule with CRUD endpoints (admin-only writes) and GET /skills public read.
Add GET /skills/:id/progress endpoint returning per-user mastery %.
frontend_changes: New Skills page (/skills) with skill card grid.
Skill card shows: icon, name, completion bar, locked/unlocked state.
Clicking a skill card launches quiz pre-filtered to that skill's due words.

features.name: Progress Dashboard
features.description: A personal stats page aggregating learning history: words by SRS status, streak, XP over time, accuracy rate, and total reviews. Backed entirely by existing SRS data.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see my learning statistics so that I can feel a sense of progress and identify areas that need more work.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows: current streak, longest streak, total words (new/learning/mastered), daily XP chart (last 7 days), overall accuracy %, and total reviews completed.
Word status definitions: new = never reviewed, learning = interval < 21 days, mastered = interval >= 21 days.
XP chart is a bar chart with one bar per day for the past 7 days.
Accuracy % = total correct / total reviews across all time.
Page loads within 2 seconds on a cold session.
backend_changes: GET /srs/progress already returns per-word data — add a summary endpoint GET /srs/stats returning aggregate counts and XP series.
frontend_changes: New Dashboard page (/dashboard) linked from nav.
StatsCard components for streak, word counts, accuracy.
Simple bar chart for XP (can use a lightweight library or CSS bars).

features.name: Achievement Badges
features.description: One-time awards for reaching milestones. Badges are derived from existing data and stored as a list of earned achievement IDs on the user record.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to earn badges for milestones so that small wins feel rewarding.
acceptance_criteria: Initial badge set: First Review, 7-Day Streak, 30-Day Streak, 100 Words Learned, 500 Reviews, 50 XP in a Day, First Skill Completed.
Badges are awarded server-side at review submission time when the condition is first met.
Earned badges are visible on the user profile page with name, icon, and date earned.
A toast notification appears in the frontend when a new badge is earned (detected via API response flag).
backend_changes: Add earnedAchievements: { achievementId: string, earnedAt: Date }[] to User schema.
Create achievements definition map (id, name, description, condition function).
Check and award badges inside POST /srs/review handler.
Include newlyEarnedAchievements in the review response payload.
frontend_changes: Achievements section on profile/dashboard page.
Toast notification component triggered by newlyEarnedAchievements in review response.

features.name: Pronunciation Audio
features.description: Attach audio clips to words so learners can hear correct Uzbek pronunciation. A speaker icon in the quiz and word-list views plays the clip. Optionally, a listen-and-choose exercise plays audio and asks the user to select the written word.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to hear how words are pronounced so that I learn correct spoken Uzbek, not just reading.
acceptance_criteria: Word schema has an optional audioUrl (string) field.
In the quiz, a speaker icon appears when audioUrl is set; clicking it plays the audio.
Audio auto-plays when a new question is shown (configurable in user settings).
Admin interface (or seeding script) can attach audio URLs to words.
If audioUrl is absent, the speaker icon is hidden (no broken audio states).
backend_changes: Add audioUrl (string, optional) field to Word schema.
Include audioUrl in all word query responses.
frontend_changes: Add AudioButton component that wraps the HTML Audio API.
Render AudioButton in Translation component when audioUrl is present.
Add 'auto-play audio' toggle to user settings.
Optional: new ListenAndChoose exercise type component.

features.name: Mistake Review Mode
features.description: After a quiz session the results screen offers a 'Review Mistakes' button that re-runs only the words the user answered incorrectly. No backend changes required.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to immediately re-practice words I got wrong so that I reinforce correct answers while the session is fresh.
acceptance_criteria: The quiz results screen shows which words were answered incorrectly.
A 'Review Mistakes' button is enabled only when at least one answer was wrong.
Clicking it starts a new quiz session pre-loaded with only the incorrect words.
The mistake-review session does not submit results to /srs/review (it is unscored practice).
frontend_changes: Results screen lists incorrect answers with correct translations.
Add 'Review Mistakes' button to results screen that reinitialises useTimedQuizMachine with filtered word list.
Pass an unscored flag to suppress review submission in mistake-review mode.

features.name: Leaderboards
features.description: Weekly XP leaderboard among users the learner follows. Shows rank, name, avatar, and weekly XP. Resets every Monday. A lightweight first version shows only followed users; leagues can be a future iteration.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see how my weekly XP compares to friends so that friendly competition keeps me engaged.
acceptance_criteria: Users can follow/unfollow other users by user ID or username.
A leaderboard page shows weekly XP for the current user and all users they follow, ranked descending.
The weekly period resets at 00:00 UTC every Monday.
Leaderboard data is cached and refreshed at most every 5 minutes.
If the user follows nobody, the page shows a prompt to invite friends.
backend_changes: Add following: string[] (user IDs) to User schema.
Create POST /users/follow/:id and DELETE /users/follow/:id endpoints.
Create GET /leaderboard/weekly endpoint returning ranked list of followed users + self with weeklyXP.
frontend_changes: New Leaderboard page (/leaderboard).
LeaderboardRow component showing rank, avatar, name, weekly XP bar.
Follow/unfollow button on user profile pages.

features.name: Contextual Example Sentences
features.description: Add optional example sentences to each word. Sentences are shown on the quiz answer-reveal screen to demonstrate the word in context.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to see a word used in a sentence so that I understand its meaning and usage beyond a bare translation.
acceptance_criteria: Word schema has an optional exampleSentences: string[] field (Uzbek sentence + English translation pairs).
During the quiz answer-reveal phase, if exampleSentences is non-empty, one is displayed below the translation.
Sentences are visible in a word detail view (if/when one is built).
The field is optional; absence does not affect quiz flow.
backend_changes: Add exampleSentences: { uz: string, en: string }[] (optional) to Word schema.
Include exampleSentences in word query responses.
frontend_changes: Render one exampleSentences entry in the answer-reveal phase of TimedVocabularyTest.
Style it distinctly from the word/translation (smaller font, italic).

features.name: Daily Reminder Notifications
features.description: Send a push or email notification to users who have not reviewed that day at their configured reminder time. Requires a notification service and a backend cron job.
features.user_story: As a learner, I want to receive a daily reminder if I have not studied yet so that I do not break my streak by forgetting.
acceptance_criteria: Users can set a reminder time (HH:MM local time) and enable/disable reminders in settings.
A daily cron job identifies users who have enabled reminders and have not submitted a review today.
Notifications are sent via web push (service worker) or email.
Notification includes the user's current streak count and a link to the app.
Users can unsubscribe from notifications without disabling other settings.
backend_changes: Add notificationEnabled (boolean), notificationTime (string HH:MM), pushSubscription (object) to User schema.
Integrate a push notification library (e.g., web-push) or transactional email service (e.g., Resend).
Add a cron job (e.g., via @nestjs/schedule) that fires at each unique reminder time and dispatches notifications.
frontend_changes: Notification settings section in user settings page.
Register service worker and request push notification permission.
Save push subscription object via PATCH /users/me.
